text.title: mcp-transport-stdio-vs-http Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 high-impact failure modes for mcp-transport-stdio-vs-http with detectors and fixes that map back to rules in 01-core-rules.xml.

antipattern.symptom: Tried to ship stdio over a TCP socket
antipattern.root-cause: Architectural confusion: stdio means subprocess, not network.
antipattern.fix: If clients are remote, transport must be streamable-http.
antipattern.detector: Try connecting from a remote client; should fail loudly.

antipattern.fix: SSE deprecated post-2026-03-26; use Streamable HTTP. Keep SSE only for back-compat clients.
antipattern.detector: Check the spec date in your codebase.

antipattern.root-cause: Auth bolted on later, then 'temporarily' disabled.
antipattern.fix: Refuse to start without OAuth 2.1; CI gate.

antipattern.root-cause: Default off in some SDK versions.
antipattern.fix: Force resumable=true; long-running tool runs need it.
antipattern.detector: Kill the network mid-tool-call; verify resume.
